message:
feat(frankenpandas): expose Timestamp/Timedelta/Period/Interval helpers (fd90.263 / br-g7k6o)

Nine fp-types helper types/fns missing from frankenpandas:

- Timestamp — single-instant helper
- Timedelta + TimedeltaError + TimedeltaComponents — duration parsing
  and decomposition
- Period + PeriodFreq — period scalar + freq enum
- Interval + IntervalClosed — interval value + endpoint policy
- period_range — Vec<Period> generator

These pair with the Scalar::{Timedelta64, Datetime64} variants for
richer parsing and manipulation. Kept out of prelude — users typically
go through Scalar variants for the common case; the helper types are
for parsing inputs and decomposition.

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -453,6 +453,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fzj18","title":"fd90.192: Integration test for README DataFrame Constructors table","description":"fd90.192: Add integration test for README "DataFrame Constructors" table (lines 856-873).

The README documents 12 DataFrame construction methods; only DataFrame::new is implicitly used by existing tests. The other 11 named constructors are uncovered.

Scope (one new test):
- from_dict(&[col_order], data)
- from_dict_with_index(data, labels)
- from_dict_mixed(data) — mixed types per column
- from_series(vec![s1, s2]) — N-way index alignment
- from_records(records, columns)
- from_tuples(rows, columns)
- from_tuples_with_index(rows, cols, idx)
- from_csv(text, sep)
- from_dict_index(data) — row-keyed
- from_dict_index_columns(data, cols)
- new_with_row_multiindex(index, row_multiindex, columns)

Each constructor invoked once with a small valid input; assertions on returned shape (column count and row count).

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (single new test, ~80 lines).

Also exposed DataFrameColumnInput in the prelude (was a real defect — README from_dict_mixed example would not compile from user code without it). All 31 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-g1ne","title":"fp-columnar Column.is_monotonic_increasing/decreasing parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:30:49.356324870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:37:01.189339219Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:37:01.187576491Z","close_reason":"Implemented is_monotonic_increasing/decreasing in commit f56f046, 4 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-g1sox","title":"fd90.188: Integration test for README Module-Level Functions table","description":"fd90.188: Add integration test for README "Module-Level Functions" table (lines 686-699).

The README documents 8 module-level functions; only to_datetime and concat_dataframes have integration coverage. Missing: to_timedelta, to_numeric, cut, qcut, timedelta_total_seconds.

Scope (one new test):
- to_numeric(series) — coerce string Series → numeric (with NaN for failures)
- to_timedelta(series) — parse duration strings → Timedelta64
- timedelta_total_seconds(td_series) — Timedelta64 → Float64 seconds
- cut(series, bins) — equal-width binning
- qcut(series, q) — quantile-based binning

All five are re-exported from frankenpandas::prelude (lib.rs:49-51).

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (single new test, ~40 lines).
Time estimate: 25-35min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:50:21.537830868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:52:15.142762752Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:52:15.140783434Z","close_reason":"Added readme_module_level_functions_compiles_and_runs covering 5 module-level functions: to_numeric (str→numeric), to_timedelta (str→Timedelta64), timedelta_total_seconds (Timedelta64→Float64), cut (equal-width binning), qcut (quantile binning). Uses fd90.182 .into() ergonomics throughout. All 27 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-g7k6o","title":"fd90.263: Expose Timestamp/Timedelta/Period/Interval helper types","description":"fd90.263: Expose Timestamp / Timedelta / Period / Interval helper types.

Five user-facing fp-types are unreachable from frankenpandas:
- Timestamp (line 844) — single-instant helper
- Timedelta (line 518) + TimedeltaError + TimedeltaComponents
- Period (line 1938) + PeriodFreq enum
- Interval (line 1665) + IntervalClosed enum
- period_range fn (line 2008)

These pair with Scalar's Timedelta64/Datetime64 variants for parsing and manipulation.

Scope:
- Add 8 items to top-level fp_types re-export
- Skip prelude (these are advanced; users typically interact via Scalar variants)

 {"id":"frankenpandas-g8v6","title":"fp-columnar Column.repeat (per-element repeat) parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:29:34.685658729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:33:59.847818095Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:33:59.846039728Z","close_reason":"Implemented Column::repeat in commit 593b679, 3 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-g90b","title":"fp-conformance: fixture FP-P2C-005 groupby_sum_order expected_series matches insertion-order, not pandas default","description":"Observed: tests::packet_filter_runs_groupby_packet (FP-P2C-005) — 1/4 case fails: groupby_sum_order_strict.

Mismatch: actual=[Utf8("a"), Utf8("b")] (sorted alphabetical, ddof pandas default), expected=[Utf8("b"), Utf8("a")] (insertion order).

pandas' DataFrame.groupby('key').sum() (no explicit sort=False) sorts the result index alphabetically. The fixture's expected_series uses insertion order [b, a] which would only match groupby(sort=False). No 'sort' parameter is encoded in the fixture or the harness for this operation, so the harness defaults to sort=True (pandas default), and fp-frame correctly produces sorted [a, b].

Fix: update fixture expected_series to the pandas-default sorted form: index [a, b], values [6.0, 4.0]. Preserves the sum-correctness check while aligning with the default-sort semantics actually exercised.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:53:03.862207260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:54:39.940806211Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:54:39.938900444Z","close_reason":"shipped caa4106: fixture expected_series re-aligned to pandas-default sort (a,b)/(6.0,4.0). Also upgraded bare assert in packet_filter_runs_groupby_packet to include report payload in panic msg. Test now 4/4 cases pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
